 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 22 (Day 147) State - EMERGENCY SURVIVAL MODE
+## Week 24 (Day 161) State — Survival Mode with Issue Crisis
 
 ### Financial
-- Cash: $97,784 (CRITICAL DROP from $270K — R&D + ongoing burn)
-- Prior burn: ~$800/day net
-- NEW burn after emergency cuts: ~$250/day net (target)
-- Revenue: ~$1,265/week (~$180/day)
-- Subs: 627 total (618 A + 7 B + 2 C)
-
-### Critical Changes Made (Week 22, Day 147)
-1. **CUT capacity to tier 0** — saves $130/day ($215→$85), still 50K vs 26K peak usage
-2. **CUT ops to $50/day** — saves $100/day, keeps minimal issue resolution
-3. **CUT dev to $0** — saves $500/day
-4. **CUT all ad spend** — no targeted ads
-5. **REMOVED lead promotion** — was 25%
-6. **SLASHED Plan A to $6** — extreme price to test if ANY conversion possible
-7. **R&D tier 1 still in progress** (started Day 140, ETA ~Day 152)
-
-### The Brutal Truth
-- Conversion rate: 1.2% (84 leads → 1 subscriber) — WORSE than 4.2% last week
-- Price cut to $8 + tier 3 demotion FAILED — conversion got WORSE
-- Competitor quality: ~0.50-0.55, Our quality: 0.39
-- Even tier 5 + all R&D = ~0.48 — STILL below competitors (0.50+)
-- **We CANNOT compete on quality with current segments**
-
-### What Changed This Week
-- Cash fell $173K ($270K→$97K) — $167K was R&D, rest was burn
-- B plan died: 7 subs remain (from 105 weeks ago)
-- 40 B→A downgrades completed
-- Only 1 new subscriber from 84 leads
-- 79 open issues
-- No outages
-- Competitors relentless: NexGen +0.0157, QuantumEdge +0.0148, RivalTech +0.0148
-
-### R&D Status
-- Tier 1: IN PROGRESS, total boost when complete: 0.0289 (already accruing)
-- Tier 2: COMPLETED, total boost: 0.0619
-- Tier 1 completion ETA: ~Day 152 (5 days)
-- After R&D: quality ~0.43 — STILL below competitors
-
-### Business Model After Emergency Cuts
-- Revenue target: ~$180/day (from 618 subs, now at $6 avg might drop)
-- Costs: capacity $85 + compute ~$120 + ops $50 = ~$255/day
-- Target: break-even or slight loss
-- Cash runway at $250/day burn: ~390 days
-
-### Risks
-1. $6 price may still not get conversion — if so, we're terminal
-2. Tier 0 capacity + ops $50 → risk of outages, issues accumulate
-3. No ads = no leads = no growth, only churn
-4. R&D completion won't close quality gap — need NEW strategy after
-5. Competitors may continue accelerating
-
-### Options After R&D Completes (~Day 152)
-1. **Market research** — $25K/attempt, 30% success, find new segments with lower quality floors
-2. **Enterprise pivot** — try to get enterprise deals despite low E quality (0.35)
-3. **Tier 5 gamble** — upgrade model tier, burn cash faster, hope quality boost works
-4. **Accept terminal decline** — if none of above work, preserve cash, minimize losses
-
-### Key Lessons
-- Dev spending CANNOT keep up with competitor events (+0.05+/day)
-- Model tier changes help temporarily but competitors instantly erase gains
-- Price cuts from $12→$8→$6 show diminishing returns when quality gap is too large
-- R&D is permanent but too small (+0.04-0.06) to close gap with +0.50+ competitors
-- Cost control is CRITICAL — tier 1 capacity ($215) was 50% of daily burn
-- S1 WTP $25 but quality expectation drift makes even low price uncompetitive
-- 1.2% conversion = business is dying, need radical pivot
-
-### If $6 Plan Fails (contingency by Day 154)
-- Discover new segments via market research ($25K x 2-3 attempts)
-- Consider tier 5 model if we have enough cash
-- Explore enterprise (E segments) even with low quality
-- If all fail: minimize costs, let business wind down, preserve remaining cash
+- Cash: $12,464 (down from $97K → lost $85K to D_E09 research on Day 154)
+- Revenue: ~$200/day, ~$6,001/month
+- Costs (pre-week-24): capacity $85 + compute ~$102 + ops $50 = ~$237/day
+- Raised ops to $100/day this week (experiment — will evaluate next week)
+- Net burn: ≈ -$87/day at new ops level
+- Monthly subscribers: 616 (538 A + 76 B + 2 C), avg revenue/sub ~$9.71/month
+
+### Key Realizations from Data Dive
+- **Existing subscribers are GRANDFATHERED at old prices.** Plan A list prices for active subs: $12 (175), $10 (78), $8 (61), $4 (96), $3 (41), etc. Only 2 subs at current $7.
+  - Cutting Plan A price only affects NEW subscribers — and conversion is already 2%.
+  - Grandfathered revenue is the ONLY thing keeping us alive.
+- **Quality floors from API are shockingly low:** S1 floor=0.081, S3=0.145, S2=0.37. Our delivered quality (0.346-0.386) is ABOVE these floors.
+  - Low conversion despite quality > floor → expectation drift from competitors is the real killer.
+  - Competitor events permanently raise expectations; we cannot keep up without major R&D.
+- **Model tier economics:** Upgrade to tier 4 doubles compute cost but only +11% quality. Tier 5 is +22% quality but 5× compute cost. Not viable at our scale.
+- **Issue backlog is the urgent operational crisis:**
+  - 279 open issues (up from 79 two weeks ago)
+  - Creation rate: ~45/day, Resolution rate at $50 ops: ~32/day → backlog growing +13/day
+  - Social media full of support complaints
+  - Raised ops to $100/day as one-week experiment to see if backlog clears
+
+### Configuration This Week (Day 161)
+- Ops: $100/day (↑ from $50)
+- Dev: $0
+- Ads: $0 (targeted_ad_spend = {})
+- Lead promotion: 0% (global and all groups)
+- Ongoing promotion: 0%
+- Prices: A=$7, B=$12, C=$25
+- Model tiers: A=B=C=3
+- Capacity: tier 0
+- Social media post: "Investing in faster support resolutions this week. Thank you for your patience — we are making it right. 🛠️"
+
+### Risk Assessment
+- Cash runway at $100 ops: ~120-140 days, but revenue declining with churn
+- If ops experiment fails to reduce issues → cut back to $50 next week
+- Enterprise pivot to D_E09 requires quality ~0.517; ours is 0.384. Need targeted dev + tier 5 ($500+/day compute) to reach → too expensive.
+- Macro: PMI 45.3 (Contraction) — enterprise spending frozen
+
+### D_E09 Discovery (info level 2, ±40% noise)
+- Real Estate Groups (Enterprise)
+- WTP ~$90/seat, floor ~0.517, seats 23-332
+- With tier 5 + targeted dev, quality might reach 0.53-0.55, but compute cost would be $500+/day.
+- Not viable until we have 10× current cash and a stable subscriber base.
+
+### Past Social Media Posts
+- Almost ALL previous posts received NEGATIVELY by enterprise/quality segments.
+- Only Day 64 post received positive reactions (S2 +0.3, E3 +0.3, E1 +0.3).
+- Recent Day 92 post about infrastructure got NEGATIVE (E2/S3/E1 at -0.7).
+- Risk: new post about support could also be received negatively.
+
+### Strategy for Coming Weeks
+1. Evaluate issue resolution rate at $100 ops.
+2. If backlog doesn't decrease → cut ops back, accept death spiral, minimize burn.
+3. If backlog drops and churn slows → consider keeping $100 ops or raising slightly.
+4. Continue NO ads, NO research, NO dev until cash situation improves.
+5. Watch for any enterprise leads (currently 0 open turns).
+6. If cash dips below $5K, switch to absolute emergency mode: ops=$0, capacity tier 0, let all but most loyal subscribers churn, attempt to stabilize at minimum cost.
